pretty affair yesterday afternoon
when the members of the North
west Literary club entertained thei'
husbands and friends at their an
nual party. The rooms of the resi-
dence were artistically decorated
for the occasion with the club col
ors, green and red, while red carna-
tons, the club flower, were used to
adorn the table. A most enjoyable
evening was spent with music and
in a social manner. At late hour
refreshments were served to the 80
guests who were present.

A large membership was present
yesterday afternoon at the meeting
of the domestic scence department
of the Woman's club, which was
held at the North Central high
school. A most interesting meeting
was conducted by Miss Rogers.
whose subject of demonstration
was food combinations.

Attractive appointments marked
the pretty affair given yesterday by
Miss Lenore Townsend in compli-
ment to Miss Gladys Labaree. At l
o'clock dainty court luncheon was
served from table prettily dec
Grated with violets. The remainder
of the afternoon was spent at
bridge, where the favor was won by
Mrs. James AlversOn of Seattle,

The members of the Emerald
club were guests of Mrs. b. N.
Jones yesterday afternoon at her
home, on Ninth avenue.


& = q


The Misses Hulda and Clara Sitter
were hostesses at farewell party
given In honor of Miss Jennie Cant
well at the Sitter home, on Spofford
avenue. last night. Miss Cantwell
leaves Monday for Seattle toStake
instruction in music at the Seattle
conservatory of music


The affair last night was most
enjoyable one, the guests being
close friends of the young lady who
Is to leave for Seattle. Refresh
ments were served by the host
eSseS.


Mrs. ]. d. Thomas was hostess
yesterday afternoon when she en-
tertained the members of the
Queen Anne Social club at her
home on Thirteenth avenue.
